steps.title: Find the linear equation from graph points
steps.content:
Reading two clear grid intersections from the line:
• At pressure $x = 2$ atm, temperature $y \approx 200$ K
• At pressure $x = 8$ atm, temperature $y \approx 800$ K

Slope: $$m = \frac{800 - 200}{8 - 2} = \frac{600}{6} = 100$$
The line passes through the origin (confirmed by the graph), so the equation is $y = 100x$.

steps.title: Evaluate at pressure x = 6 atmospheres
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 6$ into $y = 100x$:
$$y = 100(6) = 600$$
The estimated temperature is 600 kelvins. That matches choice C — C is correct.
